cells.rationale: Cross-cutting transcript ordering and lifecycle invariants make false terminal transitions a subtle correctness risk.
Atomic attempt fencing, replay determinism, and schema migration form a distributed correctness contract.
Exact cross-harness binding combines stale-event correctness with injection-safe metadata carriage across many boundaries.
This architectural consumer migration must preserve distinct policies while enforcing durable resume and dispatch fencing invariants.
Destructive cleanup requires subtle recycle-safe identity and filesystem safety invariants across restore and finalize races.
The cross-consumer adversarial matrix and read-only audit require long-horizon validation of race, mutation, and data-exposure invariants.
